title: Cantryl

"[[Death Is Remotely Possible]]"—which was part of the campaign—specifies that Cantryl was in his forties by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of its events, meaning he must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] between [[41 BBY/Legends|41 BBY]] and [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref>

[[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] '''Cantryl''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who operated as a [[Crime lord/Legends|crimeboss]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] during the early years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. He controlled a [[Cantryl's criminal organization|criminal organization]] and kept accurate [[Astrogation/Legends|navigational]] charts of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. During the years that [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]] was in charge of the region, Cantryl maintained a low profile due to the Moff's intolerance of criminal gangs within his territory.

==Biography==
Cantryl was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="DIRP" /> [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] between [[41 BBY/Legends|41 BBY]] and [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="Date" /> He eventually became the [[Crime lord/Legends|crimeboss]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]] in the remote [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. He used the honorific title of "[[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]]" and controlled an [[Cantryl's criminal organization|organization of criminals]], [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], and thugs. During the years that [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]] ruled the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in the name of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="DIRP" />—and, later, as his own personal empire after the collapse of Imperial authority in the wake of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="TSB">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref>—Cantryl kept a low profile since the Moff did not tolerate criminals working within his borders.<ref name="DIRP" />

Due to his organization's activities, Cantryl was in possession of accurate [[Astrogation/Legends|navigation]] charts of the Kathol sector that highlighted all the major [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] and [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade routes]], and he was willing to sell them for 5,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Although Cantryl claimed that the charts showed the location of [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowports]] and secret smuggler routes, they did not.<ref name="DIRP" /> In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' visited Gandle Ott during a mission to track down Sarne. The New Republic had recently [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|ousted]] the Moff from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on the planet [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], causing him to flee into the Kathol sector. During his retreat, the Moff had [[Flash-clear|purged]] the navigational data on the sector from the planet's memory banks, leaving the New Republic with incomplete data that had no information on locations beyond Gandle Ott. Cantryl was one of the possible options that the crew of the ''FarStar'' considered as a potential source for obtaining the information that they needed to continue their mission.<ref name="DIRP" />

==Personality and traits==
A slim man, Cantryl was in his forties as of<ref name="DIRP" /> 8 ABY<ref name="DIRP" /> and favored dressing in [[white]] suits. He smiled a lot, but the expression never reached his dark, glittering eyes. A businessman, he was prepared to do business with most individuals as long as they had the credits to pay. However, he was not beyond demanding large sums of money for information that he knew customers badly wanted, although prices were negotiable and he could be haggled down to a more reasonable sum. Cantryl was also not above lying about the contents of the information if he thought that it would ensure a sale. Cantryl had an understanding of the workings of a bureaucratic system, and was well-informed about activity on Gandle Ott. He had a knowledge of operating security systems, and was trained to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="DIRP" />

==Equipment==
Cantryl carried a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and often wore white suits.<ref name="DIRP" />

==Behind the scenes==
Cantryl was mentioned in ''[[Death Is Remotely Possible]]'', a [[roleplaying game]] adventure featured in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', published by [[West End Games]] as a possible source of astrogation charts that the player characters could acquire to continue their adventure.<ref name="DIRP" />

Cantryl's character does not have to be encountered in the adventure; the main aim of the players is to acquire navigational data and the adventure provides several ways for the players to obtain it. Cantryl is only one of several options, and his involvement in the scenario is dependent on the player's actions.<ref name="DIRP" />

==Appearances==
